name: The Family Room
paraphrase: Give the household a single shared room large enough for the whole family to gather in comfortably: a place where eating, talking, reading and informal play overlap. Alexander argues that without such a room the family ceases to be a group, because the members retreat to their own bedrooms and never meet except in passing; the family room therefore needs to be the largest, most welcoming common space, with seating arranged so that several activities can co-exist around a common center.
